title: Behold the First Realistic Depiction of the Human Face (Circa 25,000 BCE)
description:
in Art, History |   	March 2nd, 2026  Leave a Comment
	
			

	

?si=HOvgnTtB4xSNqpNE
In 1894, archaeВӯolВӯoВӯgist Гүdouard Piette disВӯcovВӯered the вҖңVenu
body:
in Art, History |   	March 2nd, 2026  Leave a Comment
	
			

	

?si=HOvgnTtB4xSNqpNE
In 1894, archaeВӯolВӯoВӯgist Гүdouard Piette disВӯcovВӯered the вҖңVenus of BrassemВӯpouy,вҖқ othВӯerВӯwise known as the вҖңLady with the Hood.вҖқ Unearthed in southВӯwestВӯern France and datВӯing to around 25,000В BCE, this carvВӯing repВӯreВӯsents the earВӯliВӯest realВӯisВӯtic depicВӯtion of a human face. The figureвҖҷs foreВӯhead, nose, and brows are careВӯfulВӯly carved in relief, as is the hair, arranged in a neat geoВӯmetВӯric patВӯtern. But what hapВӯpened to the mouth? Or the eyes? WeвҖҷre not sure.
The Venus is carved from mamВӯmoth ivory, likeВӯly using a stone flint, and stands just 3.65В cm tall. For some, it marks a major develВӯopВӯment in figВӯuВӯraВӯtive art. Or, as hisВӯtoВӯriВӯan Simon Schama has sugВӯgestВӯed, this figВӯurine may well be the вҖңdawn of the idea of beauВӯtyвҖқ in human culВӯture.
RelatВӯed ConВӯtentВ 
The WorldвҖҷs OldВӯest Cave Art, DisВӯcovВӯered in IndoneВӯsia, Is at Least 67,800 Years Old
AlgerВӯian Cave PaintВӯings SugВӯgest Humans Did MagВӯic MushВӯrooms 9,000 Years Ago
Was a 32,000-Year-Old Cave PaintВӯing the EarВӯliВӯest Form of CinВӯeВӯma?
A StylВӯish 2,000-Year-Old Roman Shoe Found in a Well
ExquisВӯite 2300-Year-Old ScythiВӯan WomanвҖҷs Boot PreВӯserved in the Frozen Ground of Siberia

title: The Greatest Double Agent Ever: How a Spanish Chicken Farmer Became the Most Important Double Agent in WWII
description:
in History |   	March 2nd, 2026  1 Comment
	
			

	

Juan Pujol GarВӯcГӯa was one of the rare indiВӯvidВӯuВӯals whose parВӯticВӯiВӯpaВӯtion in World War II m
body:
in History |   	March 2nd, 2026  1 Comment
	
			

	

Juan Pujol GarВӯcГӯa was one of the rare indiВӯvidВӯuВӯals whose parВӯticВӯiВӯpaВӯtion in World War II made him a MemВӯber of the Order of the British Empire and earned him the Iron Cross. He gained that unlikeВӯly disВӯtincВӯtion in perВӯhaps the riskiВӯest of all roles in espiВӯonage, that of a douВӯble agent. Despite ultiВӯmateВӯly workВӯing for the Allied cause, he creВӯatВӯed an elabВӯoВӯrate ficВӯtionВӯal perВӯsona вҖ” comВӯplete with an inventВӯed spy netВӯwork operВӯatВӯing across Great Britain вҖ” who proВӯfessed loyВӯalВӯty to the Nazi cause. Not only did Pujol get this charВӯacВӯter plugged into the real GerВӯman intelВӯliВӯgence sysВӯtem, he also got him on its payВӯroll, receivВӯing what came to the equivВӯaВӯlent of more than $6 milВӯlion in todayвҖҷs U.S. dolВӯlars for supВӯplyВӯing inforВӯmaВӯtion вҖ” inforВӯmaВӯtion that ultiВӯmateВӯly conВӯtributed to the AxisвҖҷ loss of the war.
The stoВӯry of how this chickВӯen farmer from Barcelona became the most imporВӯtant douВӯble agent of World War II is told in the aniВӯmatВӯed PriВӯmal Space video above. Unlike many of the spies hisВӯtoВӯry has rememВӯbered more clearВӯly, Pujol didВӯnвҖҷt begin his espiВӯonage career in the employ of any govВӯernВӯment in parВӯticВӯuВӯlar.
RadВӯiВӯcalВӯized, if that be the word, by the expeВӯriВӯence of havВӯing been draftВӯed into the SpanВӯish CivВӯil War, he vowed to dedВӯiВӯcate his life to вҖңthe good of humanВӯiВӯty.вҖқ Turned away by the British embassy, to which heвҖҷd offered his serВӯvices because Britain opposed Nazi GerВӯmany, he went freeВӯlance, re-inventВӯing himВӯself as a Third Reich-loyВӯal SpanВӯish milВӯiВӯtary man seekВӯing an assignВӯment in the U.K. TakВӯen on by GerВӯmany, he instead decamped to LisВӯbon, where he began manВӯuВӯfacВӯturВӯing ersatz intelВӯliВӯgence reports using newsВӯreel footage and tourist brochures.
HowВӯevВӯer makeshift, PujolвҖҷs craft proved impresВӯsive to both GerВӯmany and Britain, which launched an interВӯnaВӯtionВӯal spy hunt for him. He thus accomВӯplished his goal of becomВӯing an offiВӯcial British douВӯble agent, in which capacВӯiВӯty he arrived at his finest hour: misВӯleadВӯing the GerВӯmans as to the 1944 вҖңDвҖ‘DayвҖқ invaВӯsion of NorВӯmandy in an effort called OperВӯaВӯtion ForВӯtiВӯtude. In SpanВӯish, that would be ForВӯtВӯaleza, which became the title of an RTVE docВӯuВӯmenВӯtary about PujolвҖҷs long-untold stoВӯry a few years ago. But if any sinВӯgle word reflects PujolвҖҷs conВӯtriВӯbuВӯtion to hisВӯtoВӯry, that word must be GarВӯbo, the code name assigned him by his first British case offiВӯcer. After all, what othВӯer name вҖ” at least in 1942 вҖ” could quite so evocaВӯtiveВӯly befit an agent whose skills of craftВӯing and inhabВӯitВӯing inventВӯed charВӯacВӯters made his hanВӯdlers regard him as вҖңthe best actor in the worldвҖқ?
RelatВӯed conВӯtent:
The CIAвҖҷs ForВӯmer Chief of DisВӯguise Show How Spies Use CosВӯtumes in UnderВӯcovВӯer OperВӯaВӯtions
The StoВӯry of ElizeВӯbeth FriedВӯman, the PioВӯneerВӯing CrypВӯtolВӯoВӯgist Who ThwartВӯed the Nazis & Got Burned by J. Edgar Hoover
The French Designed a Fake RepliВӯca of Paris to Fool GerВӯman Bombers DurВӯing World War I
DisВӯcovВӯer the CIAвҖҷs SimВӯple SabВӯoВӯtage Field ManВӯuВӯal: A TimeВӯless Guide to SubВӯvertВӯing Any OrgaВӯniВӯzaВӯtion with вҖңPurВӯposeВӯful StuВӯpidВӯiВӯtyвҖқ (1944)
The CIAвҖҷs RecВӯtal Tool Kit for Spies вҖ” CreВӯatВӯed for TruВӯly DesВӯperВӯate SitВӯuВӯaВӯtions DurВӯing The Cold War
Based in Seoul,В ColВӯinВ MarshallВ writes and broadВӯcasts on cities, lanВӯguage, and culВӯture. HeвҖҷs the author of the newsletВӯterВ Books on CitiesВ as well as the booksВ н•ңкөӯ мҡ”м•Ҫ кёҲм§ҖВ (No SumВӯmaВӯrizВӯing Korea) andВ KoreВӯan Newtro.В FolВӯlow him on the social netВӯwork forВӯmerВӯly known as TwitВӯter atВ @colinmarshall.

title: Radioposter Launches Paper-fi: Analog Books with Synchronized Soundtracks
description: For all the talk about 2026 being the year of analog, most of the conversation has been about rediscovering old formats like vinyl, film photograp
body:
For all the talk about 2026 being the year of analog, most of the conversation has been about rediscovering old formats like vinyl, film photography, and journaling. WhatвҖҷs been missing from these hobbies is something genuinely new, a reason to believe paper can do more than it did before. Radioposter is betting it can.В 
The Midwest-based startup has built what it calls Paper-fi: physical books with synchronized audio soundtracks that follow readers in real time as they turn each page. No chips embedded in the paper, no QR codes to scan. The system uses patented computer vision and other modes through a smartphone or smart glasses to track your place in the book and play the corresponding audio. Whether music, ambient sound, or narration, the soundtrack moves with you at your own pace. The books themselves are designed more like art objects than traditional reading material, showcasing highly visual stories where audio and imagery carry the narrative together. Think coffee table book meets documentary or movie score.В 

Sound design, composition, illustration, photography, and storytelling all collapse into a single physical object. ItвҖҷs not an audiobook. ItвҖҷs not a picture book with a playlist. ItвҖҷs a format that didnвҖҷt exist before, one in which image, sequence, and sound interact on paper in ways that screens have made us forget.
After years of infinite scroll and algorithmically generated content, people are looking for more tangible hobbies. The appetite for objects you can hold, experiences that have edges and endings, is real. Radioposter is positioning Paper-fi as a way to give the medium new superpowers, making analog competitive again without abandoning what makes internet storytelling compelling.
The company publishes its own titles and the app that makes it all work. Two books have launched already, with more in development. They are also actively looking for artists who want to pitch their work for the format and have programs to support publishing end-to-end.
Whether Paper-fi becomes a lasting medium or a beautiful experiment, it represents something rare: a new analog artifact built for this moment, not borrowed from the past.
Learn more at radioposter.com.

schema.headline: Radioposter Launches Paper-fi: Analog Books with Synchronized Soundtracks

title: Maximum's Upcycled 'Billex' Process Turns Discarded Bank Notes into Furniture
description: Money manufacturers the world over are forever contending with counterfeiters. Before the U.K. introduced a new pound coin in 2017, for example, t
body:
Money manufacturers the world over are forever contending with counterfeiters. Before the U.K. introduced a new pound coin in 2017, for example, the earlier version was easy enough to fake that there were tens of millions of fraudulent copies in circulation. The same goes for paper bank notes, which over the years have been printed with increasingly high-tech features such as holograms, watermarks, and distinctive material blends. More recently, many countries have also implemented plastic coatings.
Banque de France, a central money-producing outfit in Europe, has adopted a technology called EverFit, which includes a polymer coating on cotton-blend notes that increases durability. Every year, up to three billion notes may be printed, but they must adhere to the strictest standards of technical quality. If they donвҖҷt, theyвҖҷre shredded. One drawback, though, is that theyвҖҷre not recyclable. ThatвҖҷs where Paris-based furniture design studio Maximum saw a unique opportunity.

MaximumвҖҷs unique line of stools, called Billex, repurposes the masses of discarded bills into modern, functional objects. With a cotton substrate and two layers of plastic coating, the tiny fragments can be manipulated with heat and compressed into a hard shape. Not only is the stool a useful and stylish design piece unto itself, produced in a variety of colors, but the Billex concept is also something of a prototype. It can be employed for a wide range of applications, as the shredded notesвҖ”and the stools themselvesвҖ”can be upcycled and compressed into virtually any shape.
See more on MaximumвҖҷs Instagram.

schema.headline: Maximum's Upcycled 'Billex' Process Turns Discarded Bank Notes into Furniture

title: Ruby Silvious' Tiny Paintings and Assemblages Reimagine Used Teabags
description: From diminutive pieces of fine mesh, Ruby Silvious meticulously conjoins, stains, tears, and sculpts a range mixed-media collages and tiny paintin
body:
From diminutive pieces of fine mesh, Ruby Silvious meticulously conjoins, stains, tears, and sculpts a range mixed-media collages and tiny paintings from repurposed teabags. She often incorporates other elements, such as sardine tins, thread spools, and cut paper elements. The bags are emptied and flattened and sometimes connected to form longer surfaces, often keeping the strings and tabs attached. Other times, Silvious further manipulates the soft paper substrate, creating life-size garments that possess an ethereal, even spectral quality.
This spring, SilviousвҖҷ work can be seen in group shows in Vermont, Maryland, Philadelphia, Massachusetts, and New York. During the summer, she will be collaborating with MГЎlaga-based artist David Seaton, whose abstract paintings inspired some of SilviousвҖҷ recent paper pieces, followed by a show of both of their work in Rhonda, Spain. And in the fall, her pieces will also be included in a group exhibition at Gallery of Hermosa. See more and follow updates on Instagram.

schema.headline: Ruby Silvious' Tiny Paintings and Assemblages Reimagine Used Teabags

title: In Conversation: Will Wilson
description:
Announcement
                In partnership with Art Bridges, the Visual Arts Center of New Jersey presents works by Will Wilson alon
body:
Announcement
                In partnership with Art Bridges, the Visual Arts Center of New Jersey presents works by Will Wilson alongside historic photographs by Edward Sheriff Curtis. On view through August 23.
            
                
                                
                            
                
            
        
            Will Wilson, Will Wilson, Citizen of the Navajo Nation (2013), Trans-customary DinГ© Artist, printed 2018, archival pigment print from wet plate collodion scan, 22 x 17 inches (courtesy of Art Bridges, photo by Brad Flowers)In partnership with Art Bridges, the Visual Arts Center of New Jersey is pleased to present In Conversation: Will Wilson, on view in the Main Gallery through Sunday, August 23, 2026. The exhibition brings together contemporary photographs by DinГ© (Navajo) artist Will Wilson (b. 1969) and historic images by Edward Sheriff Curtis.WilsonвҖҷs work reexamines how Native peoples have been photographed and represented over time. Using modern photographic techniques and digital media, he responds to CurtisвҖҷs influential project The North American Indian (1907вҖ“1930), inviting viewers to reflect on questions of identity, visibility, and who has the power to shape the images we see. Through this dialogue between past and present, In Conversation offers a thoughtful and engaging look at photographyвҖҷs role in shaping cultural narratives.Running alongside this exhibition, the Visual Arts Center will also present Present and Growing, a solo exhibition by Gabriel Garcia Roman, in the Mitzi and Warren Eisenberg Gallery. Additionally, With Passing Years, featuring work by Chrystofer Davis, will be on view in the MaritГ© and Joe Robinson Strolling Gallery I through June 7, 2026.To learn more, visit artcenternj.org.

title: Curator Diya Vij Named NYC Culture Commissioner
description:
News
								The veteran art worker is a вҖңvisionary and deeply thoughtful leader,вҖқ  said Mayor Mamdani.
body:
News
								The veteran art worker is a вҖңvisionary and deeply thoughtful leader,вҖқ  said Mayor Mamdani.
						
								
																
														
								
						
		
	  		    
        
            Curator Diya Vij (photo by Sam Richardson)
    
		
				
						Diya Vij, vice president of curatorial and arts programs at the Brooklyn nonprofit Powerhouse Arts, will be New York City's next culture commissioner.  Rumors about Vij's pick for the top job at the Department of Cultural Affairs (DCLA) have circulated in the local art community for weeks, until the New York Times broke the news today, February 28.Vij, who was a member of Mayor Zohran Mamdani's transition team, will succeed Laurie Cumbo just four months after joining Powerhouse Arts. вҖңThis administration has renewed my belief that city government can be a site of real change вҖ” and that art and culture are essential to that project,вҖқ saidВ Vij in a statement today.В вҖңToo many artists have been forced out of the city they love вҖ” crushed by the cost-of-living crisis. As Commissioner, I will extend the MayorвҖҷs affordability agenda to arts and culture. It is an honor to help build a city where artists, cultural workers, and New Yorkers across all five boroughs can do more than get by вҖ” they can live full, vibrant and curious lives.вҖқAn experienced curator with a community-forward approach, she has held several key positions at Creative Time, the High Line, and elsewhere. She has even worked at the DCLA from 2014 to 2019.  Vij got her start as a curatorial fellow at the Queens Museum in 2010, where she worked under the leadership of Tom Finkelpearl, who later served as culture commissioner under Mayor Bill de Blasio.вҖңI feel grateful all the time that it was my entry point into the art world because it was always about understanding how art and community live together and having a blurry understanding of the limits of the fields of art,вҖқ she told Hyperallergic in a 2021 podcast interview. вҖңI'm always working around the assumption, idea, and understanding that art is everywhere," she added. "The artists are everywhere...they're in and out of different industries and movement spaces, and part of coalition building.вҖқWhen Finkelpearl was tapped as commissioner in 2014, he brought Vij with him. Together with the department's then-Chief of Staff Shirley Levy, she launched the Public Artists in Residence (PAIR) program in 2015, nestling artists within city agencies for a year-long residency to вҖңpropose and implement creative solutions to pressing civic challenges.вҖқCreative TimeвҖҷs Diya Vij Helps Launch an Art World Think TankThe unorthodox program will gather together a group of thought leaders for 10 months to reflect and advise on happenings in the art community.HyperallergicHrag VartanianAt Creative Time, Vij spearheaded the organization's CTHQ вҖ” a permanent event space in Manhattan's Lower East Side that functions as a collaborative hub for artists and activists working in social, economic, and accessibility justice. In that same vein, she launched the organization's Research & Development Fellowship for socially engaged artists and re-ignited the Creative Time Summit in 2024 after a five-year hiatus. Among other major projects, Vij facilitated the Indigenous-led public exhibition The WorldвҖҷs UnFair (2023) and organized Moving Chains: Toward Abolition (2023), a critical day-long program on Governor's Island.In a statement to the Times, MayorВ Mamdani called Vij a вҖңvisionary and deeply thoughtful leader who understands that art is not ornamental to this city вҖ” it is essential to it.вҖқHe continued: вҖңUnder DiyaвҖҷs leadership, we will fight to keep New York a city where artists can afford to live and create, and where every New Yorker, in every borough, can experience the energy and inspiration that makes art possible.вҖқ
									
											
												News
											
											
												Diya Vij
											
											
												New York
											
											
												New York City Department of Cultural Affairs

schema.headline: Curator Diya Vij Named NYC Culture Commissioner
